title: Tests: SOCKS5 proxy — unit and integration
description:
Write the unit and integration tests for the SOCKS5 proxy feature covering proxy URL parsing, socks5h vs socks5 path selection, .onion detection, and connection error handling.

## What
Independent test suite for the SOCKS5 proxy feature using external oracles (RFC 1928 wire format, tokio-socks crate behavior).

## Test oracle rule
Do NOT use the proxy code under test as its own oracle. Tests must verify behavior against known-correct expected values or by integration with an actual TCP listener.

## Tests to write

### Unit tests (in core/src/transport.rs or a test module)

1. proxy_url_parsing_socks5h: parse 'socks5h://127.0.0.1:9050' → scheme=socks5h, host=127.0.0.1, port=9050
   Oracle: url::Url::parse behavior is the oracle

2. proxy_url_parsing_with_credentials: parse 'socks5://user:pass@192.168.1.1:1080' → correct host/port extraction, credentials not exposed in any error message
   Oracle: url::Url::parse

3. proxy_scheme_rejection: scheme 'http' must be rejected with error containing 'socks5'
   Oracle: expected error message string

### Integration tests (require a live TCP listener as mock SOCKS5 server or use the reject-at-TCP-level test)

4. proxy_unreachable_gives_clear_error: attempt connect with proxy=Some('socks5h://127.0.0.1:19999') to any relay → Err, error message contains 'SOCKS5 connect failed'
   Oracle: tokio_socks connection to a closed port returns Err; test verifies error propagates with correct context string

5. direct_connect_still_works: proxy=None behaves identically to before (regression guard)
   Oracle: existing e2e test behavior

### CLI validation tests (in cli/src/main.rs or integration test)

6. onion_no_proxy_warn: run nie with .onion relay URL and no proxy → warn! message appears in stderr/tracing output
   Oracle: expected warn string

7. onion_socks5_warn: .onion relay + socks5:// proxy → warn! about DNS leak
   Oracle: expected warn string

8. onion_socks5h_no_warn: .onion relay + socks5h:// proxy → no warning
   Oracle: absence of warn string

## Algorithm
1. Add unit tests to core/src/transport.rs in a #[cfg(test)] module
2. Add integration tests to relay/tests/ or core/tests/ using tokio::test
3. For integration test #4: spawn a tokio TcpListener on localhost:19999, immediately drop it (so port is closed), then call connect() with proxy pointing to that port
4. For CLI tests: test the validation logic as a function (extract the validation into a testable fn validate_proxy_url(proxy, relay) -> Result<Option<Warnings>>)

## Defensive programming rules
- Tests must not call sleep() — use barriers or join handles
- Never modify tests to make them pass — if a test fails, report it back to the implementer bead
- Test vectors for URL parsing must be hard-coded strings, not generated from the code under test

## Agent team
Researcher (read implemented code from W2A + W2B), Tester (write test cases from spec — no peeking at impl)

Acceptance: cargo test --workspace exits 0; clippy exits 0; all 8 test cases pass.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace exits 0; clippy exits 0; all named test cases are present and pass
